anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Dzogchen or Mahamudra luminous selfless awareness, plus Pseudo-Dionysius's claim that the divine Cause is beyond both affirmation and negation.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence and pushes the idea away from candidate discovery toward extended synthesis.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: Luminous selfless awareness can look neither ownerless in a simple Bahiya sense, nor owned by an Atman, nor addressed to a devotional recipient. Dionysius also makes the Christian recipient unstable, because the final move refuses both affirmation and denial of God as a graspable object of intent.

claim_units: The practical movement is loosening attention from objects and ordinary self-story.
Udana 1.10 makes the sense fields sufficient and denies any locatable you there.
Brihadaranyaka Upanishad 3.7.23 makes the unseen seer the sole knower and inner Self.
The Cloud of Unknowing directs thought and will away from creatures toward God through naked intent.
The model predicts that traditions train a post-negation custody rule, not only a doctrine of self or no-self.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Programs with similar decentering exercises should diverge in outcomes and language according to the post-decentering recipient they train.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: De-identification practices loosen ordinary ownership of thoughts, then assign the released stance to no-self mindfulness, observing self, values, therapist relationship, or a higher power.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If de-identification outcomes and reports are unaffected by ownership or recipient framing, the custody structure is probably only a theological gloss.

exact_claim: After object and self-story loosen, traditions differ by where the freed attention is allowed to belong: no owner, witnessing Self, or God.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Interview transcripts should cluster around no one there, witnessing Self, and God or beloved as recipient, even when sensory content and quietness descriptions overlap.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Practitioners trained in Bahiya-style sense instruction, Advaita self-inquiry, and Christian apophatic prayer should diverge most in ownership and recipient language at the moment ordinary self-reference drops.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Micro-phenomenological interviews with trained Theravada, Advaita, and apophatic practitioners.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Reports cluster only by phenomenological texture, emotional tone, or teacher charisma, with no stable ownership or recipient pattern.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: A practitioner given no-location language, witness language, or God-directed language after similar objectless calm should later describe different belonging or authorization, while reporting similar quiet or contentlessness.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Changing the instruction at the handoff point should change later interpretation more than immediate perceptual content.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Controlled meditation instruction study with post-session interview coding.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: The initial phenomenology changes before doctrinal instruction, or the ownership language remains random after instruction.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Texts should pair negation of objects or self-story with instructions such as do not locate a self, know the unseen seer, or lift the heart to God.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Primary manuals should contain explicit or implicit custody rules near passages that loosen ordinary selfing.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Close-reading corpus of Theravada, Advaita, Christian apophatic, Dzogchen, and Mahamudra manuals.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: The relevant manuals loosen objects without any subsequent owner, Self, or recipient language, or use all three interchangeably.

literature_search_queries: objectless awareness Advaita Buddhist Christian apophatic
pure consciousness event Forman Advaita Buddhism Christian mysticism objectless
attention Cloud of Unknowing Bahiya Atman
Fasching I Am of the Nature of Seeing witness-consciousness Buddhist Advaita
pure awareness experience Ramm 2023 witness consciousness selfless
minimal phenomenal experience objectless selfless awareness Buddhist Advaita

near_neighbors.difference: It sorts experiences and doctrines, not the handoff of loosened attention or ownership permissions in practice.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The new model is close to a practice-language recoding of established mystical-experience typologies.
near_neighbors.overlap: Already distinguishes Advaita identity with Brahman, theistic union or communion with God, Buddhist unconstructed experience, apophatic negation, and common-core versus constructivist interpretations.
near_neighbors.source: Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy, Mysticism, sections on union, apophatic mysticism, perennialism, Stace, Smart, and Zaehner, https://plato.stanford.edu/archives/win2023/entries/mysticism/

near_neighbors.difference: Forman tends to argue for direct encounter with consciousness itself, while this idea emphasizes divergent custodians of attention after ordinary ownership weakens.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. Forman already frames the central terrain as contentless consciousness plus interpretive difference.
near_neighbors.overlap: Forman's pure consciousness event and dualistic mystical state compare objectless or nonintentional awareness across Indian, Chinese, medieval European, and modern accounts.
near_neighbors.source: Robert K. C. Forman, Mysticism, Mind, Consciousness, Google Books page, https://books.google.com/books/about/Mysticism_Mind_Consciousness.html?id=I28wCczdcpEC

near_neighbors.difference: Does not triangulate with Bahiya's no-location instruction or Christian apophatic love.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. One major unit of the model is already well articulated.
near_neighbors.overlap: Gives a close Advaita account of witness-consciousness as the abiding dimension in which changing experiences appear, directly overlapping the hidden-seer custody leg.
near_neighbors.source: Wolfgang Fasching, I Am of the Nature of Seeing: Phenomenological Reflections on the Indian Notion of Witness-Consciousness, PhilPapers, https://philpapers.org/rec/FASIAO-5

near_neighbors.difference: Does not classify traditions by where freed attention may belong.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. It covers the phenomenological base, but not the custody typology.
near_neighbors.overlap: Examines meditative reports of awareness itself and the possibility of non-sensory phenomenal character, close to the model's post-object attention zone.
near_neighbors.source: Brentyn J. Ramm, Pure Awareness Experience, PhilPapers, https://philpapers.org/rec/RAMPAE-5

near_neighbors.difference: Sells analyzes apophatic discourse and its paradoxes, not attention ownership across Buddhist, Advaita, and Christian practices.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. It makes the Christian leg less new and highlights the Dionysian anomaly.
near_neighbors.overlap: Shows apophatic language turning back on its own propositions and names across Greek, Christian, and Islamic texts, close to the Christian unknowing side.
near_neighbors.source: Michael A. Sells, Mystical Languages of Unsaying, University of Chicago Press, https://press.uchicago.edu/ucp/books/book/chicago/M/bo3635525.html

next_loop_instructions: Close-read Dzogchen and Mahamudra instructions on rigpa or luminous awareness against Bahiya and Brihadaranyaka before publishing the custody model.
Code practitioner reports for ownership terms, recipient terms, and no-location terms instead of general contentless-awareness terms.
Search Zaehner, Stace, Ninian Smart, Forman, Katz, Pike, McGinn, and Sells before raising novelty above extended.
Ask a Christian apophatic practitioner whether love toward God is a recipient of freed attention or the starting motive of the whole practice.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.72 to about 0.58. The custody phrase appears fresh, but the structure sits close to established work on pure consciousness, constructivism, theistic versus monistic typologies, witness-consciousness, and apophasis.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say this is just the standard experience versus interpretation debate, or say attention is the wrong category and the model misses liberation, ontology, grace, or devotion.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners independently identify a decisive handoff point and report that no-owner, witness, and God-directed instructions change practice understanding.
questions: When object fixation and ordinary self-story weaken, where does your practice tell attention or awareness to rest, belong, or not belong?
Is the word custody obvious, useful, or misleading from inside your tradition?
Would changing the handoff instruction change realization, only later interpretation, or neither?
relevant_practitioners: Theravada insight teachers familiar with Bahiya and sense restraint
Advaita Vedanta self-inquiry teachers
Christian apophatic contemplative directors
Dzogchen or Mahamudra teachers
Scholars of mysticism and contemplative science

primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The term attention imports modern psychology into texts that speak about clinging, Self, inner ruler, will, love, and God. Bahiya may be about ending clinging rather than leaving attention unowned. Brihadaranyaka may be ontological rather than attentional. The Cloud may intensify desire rather than suspend ownership.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: I did not find the exact phrase or structure of attention custody across Buddhist no-self, Advaita witness, and Christian apophatic prayer. However, close prior work already distinguishes common mystical experience, culturally shaped interpretation, monistic versus theistic typologies, witness-consciousness, and pure awareness. The originality is therefore a reframing of a known comparison around practice-level ownership language, not a standalone discovery.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: The primary contrast is real but narrower than the proposed model. Udana 1.10 uses the same seeing, hearing, sensing, cognizing sequence and then removes locatable self-reference. Brihadaranyaka 3.7.23 uses seeing, hearing, perceiving, knowing to identify the sole inner ruler and Self. This supports a strong no-owner versus hidden-seer contrast. The Christian leg is less exact: The Cloud of Unknowing chapter 3 begins with love and intent toward God, rather than first freeing neutral attention and then assigning it. Pseudo-Dionysius further strains the model because Mystical Theology chapter 5 places the Cause beyond both affirmation and negation, so even God as a stable recipient is apophatically unstable. Cloud source: https://sacred-texts.com/chr/cou/cou08.htm. Dionysius source: https://hekhal.org/texts/mystical-theology-dionysius/

title: The Custody of Unclaimed Attention
unlike_statement: Unlike common-core or constructivist accounts, this idea says the decisive variable is not only whether an experience is the same or differently interpreted, but whether training assigns loosened attention to no owner, the witnessing Self, or God.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Validated-then-abandoned hybrids, especially MN 22's raft, Heart Sutra reliance on Prajnaparamita after no-attainment, and Rinzai koan curriculum after kensho.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Substantially lowers confidence in the three-box typology. The contribution survives better as a two-axis or staged model.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: These cases mix confirmation, cancellation, and dissolution across stages. The raft must be grasped properly before being released. The Heart Sutra negates attainment but retains reliance and mantra. Koan practice may exhaust conceptual striving yet becomes a formal curriculum with tests. If hybrids are normal, the three types are not stable categories.

claim_units: A method's relation to its own result may be a comparative variable upstream of doctrinal content.
Mandukya is classified as self-confirming because analysis of waking, dreaming, and deep sleep culminates in turiya as Atman to be known.
Nagarjuna, Heart Sutra, Zen koan, and Eckhart are classified as self-canceling because their tools undermine graspable thesis, wisdom, attainment, or desire.
Zhuangzi, Dzogchen, and Sufi fana are classified as self-dissolving because the practitioner or frame of method becomes irrelevant.
The type should predict verification architecture, teaching strategy, and ethical consequences.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Methods that cancel their own frame should rely more on supervision, case judgment, paradox, and indirect signs than on checklist fidelity. Methods that confirm themselves should tolerate manuals and outcome criteria better. Methods that dissolve into embodiment should prioritize apprenticeship and exemplars.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Some methods confirm themselves by producing stable results, some cancel themselves by dissolving the problem frame, and some dissolve into skilled embodiment where the practitioner no longer follows the method explicitly.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If confirmation, cancellation, and dissolution do not predict training, verification, or institutional form outside contemplative traditions, the proposed structure may be domain-specific rhetoric.

exact_claim: At completion, contemplative methods face a self-authority question: some confirm themselves, some cancel themselves, and some dissolve the practitioner beyond method-talk, with consequences for verification and transmission.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Eckhart and Nagarjuna should share more indirect, anti-possessive verification patterns with each other than Eckhart shares with more kataphatic Christian mystics. Advaita witness practice should use more direct recognition language than Madhyamaka critique.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Method-type should predict verification architecture better than tradition membership.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Coded comparison of practice manuals, teacher testing language, and post-realization instructions across at least six traditions.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Christian sources cluster together by tradition more strongly than by method-type, or all traditions use the same teacher, lineage, and conduct criteria regardless of method relation.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: After Advaita inquiry, practitioners describe recognition as stable witness or Self. After Heart Sutra or Madhyamaka practice, they resist treating any insight as an attained object. After Dzogchen or fana language, they emphasize effortless presence or transformed embodiment over method success.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Dual-trained practitioners should report different relations to their own insight when switching method-types.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Structured interviews with practitioners trained in more than one contemplative lineage.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practitioners report no stable difference, or differences track teacher personality and institutional context more than method-type.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Texts should mark when to use, trust, undermine, release, or embody a method. Examples would include raft language, preliminary versus main practice, koan curriculum before and after kensho, or thorn-removing-thorn metaphors.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Hybrid traditions should show explicit boundary management between method stages.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Historical and textual analysis of Zen, Dzogchen, Advaita, Madhyamaka, Sufi, and Christian apophatic training sequences.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: No boundary markers appear, or methods freely confirm, cancel, and dissolve without any pedagogical distinction.

therapeutic Nagarjuna Wittgenstein Tractatus ladder

Zhuangzi fish trap words meaning forget words text

near_neighbors.difference: Bilateral comparison only. It does not add self-confirming and self-dissolving alternatives or predict verification architecture across traditions.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The strongest part of the Lumenary claim, method succeeds by undoing its own authority, is already explicit prior art.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close to the self-canceling core: Nagarjuna and Wittgenstein are read as shared philosophical method without theses, aimed at dissolution of philosophical problems and soteriological disquiet.
near_neighbors.source: Joshua William Smith, Snakes and Ladders: Therapy as Liberation in Nagarjuna and Wittgenstein's Tractatus, Sophia 2021, https://ueaeprints.uea.ac.uk/id/eprint/80007/

near_neighbors.difference: Sells studies apophatic language as discourse, not a general typology of contemplative method completion.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for Eckhart and apophasis. Moderate support for the broader synthesis.
near_neighbors.overlap: Apophatic discourse is described as repeatedly turning back upon its own propositions and names, across Greek, Christian, and Islamic texts including Eckhart and Ibn Arabi.
near_neighbors.source: Michael A. Sells, Mystical Languages of Unsaying, University of Chicago Press, https://press.uchicago.edu/ucp/books/book/chicago/M/bo3635525.html

near_neighbors.difference: It is not simply canceling or dissolving. It validates proper use, then releases attachment after the crossing.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction and major anomaly. It suggests a fourth pattern or a two-axis framework.
near_neighbors.overlap: The Dhamma is a raft for crossing, to be grasped properly and then let go. This is a direct prior structure for validated-then-abandoned method.
near_neighbors.source: MN 22 Alagaddupama Sutta, raft simile, Thanissaro translation, https://www.dhammatalks.org/suttas/MN/MN22.html

near_neighbors.difference: The focus is semantic and argumentative context, not contemplative practice types or cross-tradition transmission.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the Nagarjuna unit, but it also sharpens the audit by warning against treating no-thesis as simple self-cancellation.
near_neighbors.overlap: Closely analyzes Nagarjuna's no-thesis claim and the problem of how a work full of arguments can deny having theses.
near_neighbors.source: Jan Westerhoff, The No-Thesis View: Making Sense of Verse 29 of Nagarjuna's Vigrahavyavartani, PhilPapers PDF, https://philpapers.org/archive/WESTNV.pdf

near_neighbors.difference: It shows that a self-canceling tool can become a highly structured authorization system, contrary to a simple prediction of transmission difficulty.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction and anomaly for the Zen evidence.
near_neighbors.overlap: Directly addresses the common model of koan using reason to drive itself into self-contradiction, then complicates it by describing a curriculum after kensho.
near_neighbors.source: G. Victor Sogen Hori, Koan and Kensho in the Rinzai Zen Curriculum, search-accessible copy, https://docslib.org/doc/1302437/the-koan-is-said-to-pose-to-the-ze-n-practitione-r-a-parado-x-unsolvabl-e-by-the-rational-intellectualizing-mind

near_neighbors.difference: It does not make fana one member of a cross-method typology.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. The dissolving example is known, but the comparative placement may remain useful.
near_neighbors.overlap: Already describes Sufi fana as passing away of self followed by being filled with Allah's presence, close to the dissolving pattern.
near_neighbors.source: Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy, Mysticism, discussion of fana and baqa, https://plato.stanford.edu/archives/win2023/entries/mysticism/

next_loop_instructions: Rebuild the model as two axes: validates versus undermines, and retained versus abandoned or embodied.
Close-read Mandukya, Heart Sutra, MN 22, Vigrahavyavartani 29, and Hori's koan curriculum before treating the three types as stable.
Search further for upaya, raft, ladder, thorn-removes-thorn, Gelassenheit, fana and baqa, rang grol, and practice-realization as exact structural near-neighbors.
Ask dual-trained practitioners whether the typology changes how they understand transmission, or whether it merely renames standard stage pedagogy.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.84 to about 0.64. No exact three-type typology was found in accessible searches, but the self-canceling and method-abandoning structures are well-known, and primary texts show hybrid cases that weaken the clean typology.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say every serious path already uses all three stages, or identify the model as a familiar upaya, raft, ladder, no-thesis, fana, or Gelassenheit pattern with new labels.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners find that the type distinction clarifies why different lineages test realization, authorize teachers, and warn against grasping insight in different ways.
questions: At completion, can the method still authorize its result, or must it be released, denied, or embodied?
Is confirming, canceling, dissolving an obvious distinction from inside your training, or does it distort the practice?
Do your verification methods change depending on whether the practice confirms, cancels, or dissolves itself?
relevant_practitioners: Rinzai Zen teachers familiar with koan curriculum
Madhyamaka scholars and monastic teachers
Advaita Vedanta self-inquiry teachers
Dzogchen or Mahamudra teachers
Sufi teachers familiar with fana and baqa
Christian apophatic spiritual directors

primary_pair: Mandukya Upanishad verse 7, Sanskrit and translation, https://texts.wara.in/upanishads/mukhya/mandukya/ and https://gita.pub/upanishad/mandukya/verse/7/
Heart Sutra, Sanskrit and English, https://sa.wikisource.org/wiki/प्रज्ञापारमिताहृदय and https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Translation:Shorter_Prajñāpāramitā_Hṛdaya_Sūtra
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model imposes a modern reflexive category, method's relation to itself, on traditions that may speak instead of upaya, realization, grace, direct recognition, non-grasping, or annihilation of ego. It also risks treating stage-specific pedagogies as stable types.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: I did not find an exact three-type typology of contemplative methods as confirming, canceling, and dissolving at completion. But the self-canceling portion has very strong prior art in Nagarjuna, Wittgenstein ladder readings, Buddhist raft discussions, and Sells on apophatic unsaying. The dissolving portion is also standard in fana, zuowang, and Dzogchen descriptions. The new part is the comparative axis and its predicted consequences, not the individual patterns.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: The Mandukya and Heart Sutra contrast is promising but not clean. Mandukya verse 7 negates ordinary consciousness categories, then ends by saying this is Atman and is to be known, which supports a confirming limit move. Yet Shankara's commentary also says affirmative and negative language fail, so Mandukya contains a self-canceling linguistic element. The Heart Sutra negates aggregates, sense bases, ignorance, the four truths, wisdom, and attainment, which supports the self-canceling reading. Yet it immediately says bodhisattvas rely on Prajnaparamita and gives a mantra, so the method is retained as reliance and recitation, not simply canceled. The close read supports a two-axis model better than a pure three-box typology.

title: The Method's Reckoning: What a Practice Does With Its Own Authority at Completion
unlike_statement: Unlike Smith on Nagarjuna and Wittgenstein, Sells on apophatic unsaying, or the raft and ladder motifs, this idea proposes a three-way comparative axis and claims that the axis predicts verification and transmission better than doctrine alone.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Tibetan clear-light sleep and contemporary objectless-awareness sleep reports that claim awareness during the gap, not only after it.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence from candidate discovery to extended. The anomaly does not reject the model, but it makes post-gap grammar secondary rather than foundational in some cases.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If trained practitioners can give stable, carefully interviewed reports of awareness while the gap is unfolding, then the later report is not merely assigning evidence after an uninspectable silence. The model would need to distinguish in-gap phenomenology from post-gap evidential framing.

claim_units: Post-gap reports distribute authority instead of simply preserving memory.
Advaita deep-sleep arguments weight the subject or witness in reports such as I slept and knew nothing.
Early Buddhist cessation sources weight absence, transition, conditioned development, and post-emergence inclination rather than a surviving owner.
Daoist sitting-in-forgetfulness weights transformed aftereffect rather than hidden witness continuity.
Sufi dhikr or fana language may weight the Remembered or relation over the rememberer.
The distribution of evidential weight can be used as a cross-tradition comparison variable.

cross_domain_prediction.domain: Anesthesia, blackout, trauma, and psychedelic integration reports
cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Clinical and legal settings should show different admissibility grammars for blackout reports, anesthesia emergence, trauma memory, and psychedelic ego dissolution, and these grammars should predict what is treated as evidence of continuity, harm, or transformation.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: After an interval that cannot be inspected while it occurs, communities decide whether to trust subject continuity, absence of recall, physiological transition, aftereffects, or relational testimony.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If these domains use no stable admissibility patterns, or if all rely only on external measurement regardless of later report structure, the proposed structure may be limited to contemplative rhetoric.

exact_claim: After an objectless or silent state, the first later report is not neutral memory. It is a trained admissibility grammar that assigns evidential weight to the subject, absence, transition, aftereffect, or relation.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Advaita materials should privilege witness or subject continuity; early Buddhist cessation materials should privilege cessation, transition, and fruit; Daoist zuowang should privilege aftereffect and forgetting; dhikr or fana materials should privilege relation or the Remembered.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Traditions should show stable differences in which clause of a post-gap report they treat as admissible evidence.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Blind coding of primary texts, practice manuals, and practitioner interviews using subject, absence, transition, aftereffect, relation, teacher recognition, and behavioral fruit tags.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: If evidence weights vary mainly by translator, genre, or individual teacher rather than tradition and method, the model loses predictive value.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: The same practitioner speaks differently about sleep, cessation, or objectless meditation under Advaita, Theravada, and Tibetan prompts.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Dual-trained practitioners should shift evidential weighting when switching practice frames.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Structured interviews with dual-trained Advaita, Buddhist, Sufi, Daoist, and micro-phenomenology-trained practitioners.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practitioners preserve the same evidential interpretation across frames, or report that the distinction is imposed by the interviewer.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Sanskrit, Pali, Chinese, Arabic, or Persian sources should mark subject, absence, transition, aftereffect, or relation through recurring lexical and grammatical patterns.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Original-language terms should support the proposed categories, not merely English pronoun artifacts.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Close read of Brihadaranyaka 4.3.23, Yoga Sutra 1.10, MN 44, AN 9.34, Zhuangzi zuowang passages, and primary dhikr or fana texts.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: The categories disappear or become unstable when checked against original terms and multiple translations.

AN 9.34 nothing is felt nibbana bliss cessation

near_neighbors.difference: It focuses on the Indian dreamless-sleep debate and cognitive science, not a five-category cross-tradition admissibility rubric spanning cessation, zuowang, and dhikr.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The core claim that a gap report has evidential grammar is already present in a sophisticated near-neighbor.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close. Thompson analyzes the waking sleep report as memory versus inference, compares Advaita and Nyaya, discusses the I in the report, and asks whether awareness or minimal selfhood is required across the gap.
near_neighbors.source: Evan Thompson, Dreamless Sleep, the Embodied Mind, and Consciousness, Open MIND 2015, https://open-mind.net/epubs/dreamless-sleep-the-embodied-mind-and-consciousness-the-relevance-of-a-classical-indian-debate-to-cognitive-science/OEBPS/pt03.html

near_neighbors.difference: It is empirical-adjacent and sleep-focused, not a comparative doctrinal model of admissibility rules.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction and strong anomaly source. It supports the need for a report grammar but weakens any claim that post-gap grammar is primary evidence.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. The study uses micro-phenomenological interviews on objectless awareness during sleep, notes limits of reportability, and explicitly raises whether the reporting I is added in memory rather than present during the state.
near_neighbors.source: Alcaraz-Sanchez, Awareness in the Void, Phenomenology and the Cognitive Sciences, https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11097-021-09743-0

near_neighbors.difference: The suttas do not compare themselves to Advaita, Daoism, or Sufism as alternative report grammars.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. The Buddhist report-weighting is textual prior art; the comparative framing remains the added move.
near_neighbors.overlap: The Buddhist side already gives a structural alternative to witness inference: no self-thought while entering or emerging, ordered cessation and arising of processes, and bliss named through the absence of felt experience.
near_neighbors.source: MN 44 Culavedalla Sutta and AN 9.34 Nibbanasukha Sutta, https://www.dhammatalks.org/suttas/MN/MN44.html and https://suttas.github.io/an9.34

near_neighbors.difference: It does not build a cross-tradition taxonomy of later report clauses.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to high reduction for the Advaita unit.
near_neighbors.overlap: Makes deep sleep central to Advaita Vedanta and treats susupti as a major philosophical resource for pure consciousness arguments.
near_neighbors.source: Arvind Sharma, Sleep as a State of Consciousness in Advaita Vedanta, SUNY, https://www.degruyterbrill.com/document/doi/10.1515/9780791484302/html?lang=en

near_neighbors.difference: It studies neural and phenomenological correlates of Buddhist cessation, not comparative spiritual hermeneutics of report clauses.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction for empirical-adjacent testability.
near_neighbors.overlap: Treats cessation as a discontinuity followed by reported changes in clarity and mental functioning, close to the transition and aftereffect part of the model.
near_neighbors.source: Investigation of advanced mindfulness meditation cessation experiences using EEG spectral analysis, PMC, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10843092/

next_loop_instructions: Close-read Thompson against Brihadaranyaka 4.3.23, Yoga Sutra 1.10, MN 44, and AN 9.34 before promoting the idea.
Build a coding rubric for post-gap reports with mutually exclusive and mixed tags: subject, absence, transition, aftereffect, relation, teacher recognition, behavioral fruit.
Test clear-light sleep and objectless-awareness reports as the anomaly class, using micro-phenomenological interview methods and serial awakening literature.
Check original languages and multiple translations before treating English first-person phrasing as evidence.
Ask practitioners whether the rubric changes cross-tradition interpretation or only renames familiar evidential disputes.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.71 to about 0.58. The cross-tradition admissibility rubric is useful, but the report grammar of dreamless sleep, memory, inference, witness, and post-hoc self attribution has close prior art.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say the distinction is already standard as pramana, memory versus inference, fruition review, teacher verification, or adab of remembrance, and that the new labels do not change practice.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners can use the rubric to explain why cross-tradition disputes persist even when phenomenological reports sound similar.
questions: When you hear a report after deep sleep, cessation, forgetting, or remembrance, which part of the report counts as evidence?
Is this report-weighting obvious within your training, or does naming it change how the practice is understood?
Would your tradition reject another tradition's report because it weights the wrong clause?
relevant_practitioners: Advaita Vedanta teachers using three-state analysis
Theravada teachers familiar with nirodha samapatti
Tibetan dream yoga or clear-light sleep practitioners
Daoist practitioners familiar with zuowang
Sufi teachers familiar with dhikr, fana, and baqa
Micro-phenomenology researchers of sleep and meditation

primary_pair: Brihadaranyaka Upanishad 4.3.23 with Shankara commentary, https://vivekavani.com/bru4c3v23/
MN 44 Culavedalla Sutta plus AN 9.34, https://www.dhammatalks.org/suttas/MN/MN44.html and https://suttas.github.io/an9.34
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model may make grammar carry what traditions assign to realization, teacher verification, metaphysics, ritual, or ethical fruit. Translation also matters, since first-person grammar in English may not map cleanly onto Sanskrit, Pali, Chinese, Arabic, or Persian terms.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: I did not find an exact source that classifies Advaita, Buddhist, Daoist, and Sufi post-gap reports by subject, absence, transition, aftereffect, and relation. But Evan Thompson's Open MIND essay gives a very close Advaita versus Nyaya analysis of the report I slept peacefully and I did not know anything, including memory, inference, minimal selfhood, and retrospective awareness. Alcaraz-Sanchez's micro-phenomenological study also directly studies reportability, pronoun use, and possible post-hoc self attribution in objectless sleep reports.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: Brihadaranyaka 4.3.23 treats apparent non-seeing in deep sleep as compatible with uninterrupted witness vision, because the object side has fallen away. MN 44 treats cessation through prior development, ceasing and arising fabrications, and post-emergence contact with emptiness, the signless, and the undirected. AN 9.34 makes the absence of feeling part of the reason cessation can be called pleasant. The contrast supports the proposed grammar, subject continuity versus conditioned transition and absence, but also shows that the grammar is already explicit in primary sources.

title: The Gap Report Has a Grammar
unlike_statement: Unlike Thompson on Advaita and Nyaya, or micro-phenomenology on objectless sleep reports, this idea says the evidential clause selected after a gap is a cross-tradition admissibility rule that can be coded across subject, absence, transition, aftereffect, and relation.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Other-power and direct-introduction traditions, such as Jodo Shinshu entrusting and Dzogchen pointing-out, appear to minimize self-powered agency while still functioning as paths.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence in the universal form of the claim. It supports a revised model where paths require some functional receiving capacity, but the amount and role vary sharply.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If a tradition can produce stable transformation through vow, grace, devotion, direct introduction, or teacher-field participation with very little reflexive self-management, then the minimum self may not be an architectural driver. The model would need a scale of required self-capacities rather than a universal requirement.

claim_units: Self-dissolving practice presupposes capacities for hearing instruction, effort, memory, error recognition, and self-observation.
SN 22.59 dissolves the person while preserving the causal process of liberation.
Chandogya 6.8.7 preserves the deepest Self while dissolving the reality of the student's apparent process.
Causal continuity, ontological identity, temporal collapse, and divine restoration are proposed as management strategies.
The management strategy should predict effort theory, verification, warnings, and post-practice fruit.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Therapies or pedagogies that deconstruct identity, such as trauma therapy, exposure therapy, psychedelic therapy, contemplative psychotherapy, or critical pedagogy, should have stronger screening, scaffolding, integration, and teacher-client safeguards than methods that merely add skills.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Any method that aims to dissolve or radically transform the learner's starting identity must preserve enough agency, memory, trust, and reflexivity for the method to work.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If deconstructive methods require no more scaffolding than ordinary skills training, or if safeguards do not track the degree of identity transformation, the structural claim is weakened.

exact_claim: A path that dissolves, denies, or transcends selfhood still requires a minimum functioning self, agency, memory, reflexivity, and continuity, and each tradition's strategy for managing that requirement predicts its practice architecture.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Manuals and teachers should warn against nihilism, dissociation, spiritual bypassing, false attainment, antinomianism, or ego-inflation in ways that match the path's self-management strategy.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The stronger a path's final self-dissolution claim, the more explicit its safeguards should be against premature or distorted self-loss.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Coded comparison of Theravada, Zen, Dzogchen, Advaita, Sufi, Christian apophatic, and Pure Land training materials.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Warnings show no relationship to self-teaching and track only institution, teacher personality, or general ethics.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Theravada stages, Sufi stations, and Advaita preparation should specify capacities, sequence, and error correction; sudden methods should shift burden to teacher recognition, pointing-out, vows, or post-recognition integration.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Long, effortful paths should develop more elaborate continuity mechanisms than sudden or other-power paths.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Comparative coding of practice sequences, teacher-student protocols, and attainment verification.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practice length and self-continuity machinery are unrelated, or sudden paths have equally elaborate minimum-self architecture before recognition.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Vipassana permits causal observation without ownership; Advaita permits witness or Self recognition; Soto Zen discourages gaining selfhood; Sufi practice permits surrendering agency into relation with God.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Dual-trained practitioners should report different kinds of permitted selfhood when switching methods.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Structured interviews with practitioners trained in two or more lineages.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practitioners report no stable shift, or the shifts are better explained by temperament and teacher style.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Texts and practitioners should mark investigation of the investigator, hearing tat tvam asi, fana, kensho, pointing-out, or surrender as a crisis or verification point.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The point where a method consumes its prerequisite should be specially marked as difficult, risky, or decisive.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Primary-text close reading plus practitioner interviews and retreat-report coding.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: These moments are not specially marked, or transformation is described as ordinary continuity without any self-prerequisite tension.

literature_search_queries: who practices if there is no self Buddhism liberation agency
Pudgalavada karma rebirth liberation person no self
Selfless Persons Theravada personal identity continuity denial of self Collins
you have to be somebody before you can be nobody Engler Buddhism meditation
functional self Buddhist no-self mindfulness trauma preliminary practice
minimal self meditation non-self self-pattern agency ownership
Dogen practice-realization beginner original enlightenment Bendowa
praptasya prapti attainment of the already attained Advaita Shankara

near_neighbors.difference: Engler's frame is Buddhist psychology and psychotherapy. He does not build a comparative typology of causal continuity, ontological identity, temporal collapse, and divine restoration across traditions.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The core minimum-self prerequisite is already a well-known practitioner-facing thesis.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close. Engler says meditation requires ego strength, self-observation, and affect tolerance before no-self can be safely practiced.
near_neighbors.source: Jack Engler interview, You Must Be Somebody Before You Can Be Nobody, Inquiring Mind, https://inquiringmind.com/article/0501_1_engler-interview/

near_neighbors.difference: This is an intra-Buddhist metaphysical and doctrinal dispute, not a general model of practice architecture across traditions.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The pressure between no-self and soteriological functioning is ancient, not newly discovered.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close historical prior art. Pudgalavadins argued that karma, rebirth, liberation, benevolence, and lived personhood strain a strict no-self aggregate account.
near_neighbors.source: Pudgalavada Buddhist Philosophy, Internet Encyclopedia of Philosophy, https://iep.utm.edu/pudgalavada-buddhist-philosophy/

near_neighbors.difference: The focus is Theravada discourse and imagery, not cross-tradition engineering constraints.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the Buddhist side. The Lumenary idea extends rather than originates the continuity problem.
near_neighbors.overlap: Collins directly studies how Theravada constructs personal identity and continuity on the apparently impossible basis of denying self.
near_neighbors.source: Steven Collins, Selfless Persons, summary and citation, https://buddhanature.tsadra.org/index.php/Books/Selfless_Persons

near_neighbors.difference: Clinical mindfulness and trauma are the focus, not comparative religion typology.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the functional-self and warning-profile elements.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. The article links anatta, the person, cognitive-science selfhood, functional self, trauma, and preliminary practice before transpersonal or soteriological work.
near_neighbors.source: Tempone-Wiltshire and Dowie, A Mindful Bypassing, Journal of the Oxford Centre for Buddhist Studies, https://jocbs.org/index.php/jocbs/article/view/269

near_neighbors.difference: It is a contemporary Buddhist exposition rather than a cross-tradition predictive model.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. Parts of the Lumenary wording risk being a renamed Buddhist functional-self account.
near_neighbors.overlap: Extremely close for the Buddhist unit. It speaks of functional or enactive self, minimal self, agency without ownership, and discipline through causes and conditions.
near_neighbors.source: Not-Self Sutra, Barre Center for Buddhist Studies, https://www.buddhistinquiry.org/article/not-self-sutra/

near_neighbors.difference: It is cognitive-scientific and Buddhist-psychological rather than a primary-text comparative architecture model.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to high reduction for the minimal-self and practice-transformation language.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close contemporary theory. It distinguishes minimal self, ownership, agency, self-pattern, and how meditation transforms self-processes toward selflessness.
near_neighbors.source: Pattern Theory of Selflessness, Mindfulness, https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s12671-024-02418-2

near_neighbors.difference: It does not compare this strategy against Advaita, Theravada, and Sufi models of self-continuity.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. The Zen strategy is known; the comparative placement may still be useful.
near_neighbors.overlap: The temporal-collapse strategy is already central to Dogen's practice-realization, where practice is not merely a means to later awakening.
near_neighbors.source: Dogen practice-realization sources, for example Soto and Bendowa summaries, https://zenlineage.org/schools/soto and https://buddhismnow.com/2023/10/08/practice-within-realisation-dogen/

next_loop_instructions: Recast the model as a graded variable: required receiving capacity, required agency, required memory, required reflexivity, and required post-insight integration.
Separate Buddhist two-truths and personhood prior art from the cross-tradition contribution before publication.
Add Pure Land other-power, Christian grace, and Dzogchen pointing-out as anomaly cases, not footnotes.
Ask practitioners whether the model predicts actual screening, sequencing, warning, and verification practices.

Search further in Engler, Wilber, Brown, Collins, Harvey, Duerlinger, Siderits, Albahari, Buddhist trauma literature, practice-realization scholarship, fana and baqa sources, and Advaita praptasya prapti discussions.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.74 to about 0.52. The cross-tradition architecture claim is a meaningful extension, but the minimum-self prerequisite, functional self, no-self continuity problem, and healthy ego before no-self are strong prior art.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners identify the idea as already standard: conventional person versus ultimate no-self, somebody before nobody, sadhana before recognition, practice-realization, fana and baqa, or other-power entrusting.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners find that the rubric predicts real training differences and clarifies why premature self-dissolution, clinging to witness, bypassing, or false surrender fail differently across paths.
questions: Does your path require a functional self-capacity that its final teaching later dissolves, denies, or reframes?
Is this already obvious as two truths, healthy ego before no-self, practice-realization, surrender, or grace?
Would this rubric change how you screen students, sequence practices, verify insight, or warn against errors?
relevant_practitioners: Theravada monastics and insight teachers
Advaita Vedanta teachers
Soto and Rinzai Zen teachers
Dzogchen or Mahamudra teachers
Sufi teachers familiar with fana and baqa
Pure Land teachers familiar with other-power
Buddhist psychotherapists and trauma-informed mindfulness teachers

primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model may turn a researcher's philosophical tension into a practitioner's lived problem. It also risks forcing mixed traditions into clean boxes. Theravada, Zen, Advaita, Sufism, Dzogchen, and Christian grace traditions often combine effort, surrender, recognition, teacher relation, and ethical transformation across stages.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: I did not find an exact four-strategy cross-tradition typology that predicts architecture across Buddhism, Advaita, Zen, and Sufism. But the main pressure is heavily precedented in Buddhist personhood debates, Pudgalavada arguments, Collins on selfless persons, Engler on ego strength before no-self, functional-self accounts in Buddhist psychology, and contemporary self-pattern theories of meditation.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: SN 22.59 requires monks who can listen, answer, inspect each aggregate, and become disenchanted, yet it denies that any aggregate, including consciousness, is fit to be self. Its closing grammar says the monks' minds were released, preserving process without owner. Chandogya repeatedly addresses Shvetaketu as a learner asking for more explanation, yet the repeated teaching identifies him with the Self of all this. This supports the proposed tension, but it is not new by itself. It is close to classical Buddhist debates over personhood and to Advaita pedagogy around recognizing the already present Self.

title: Every Path Needs What Its Teaching Dissolves
unlike_statement: Unlike Engler, Pudgalavada debates, Collins, or functional-self Buddhist psychology, this idea treats the minimum self as a comparative architecture variable across traditions and claims it predicts effort, verification, warnings, and fruit.
